primary_concern: Sepsis risk with acute kidney injury (AKI) and coagulopathy
clinical_summary: Elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L) combined with leukocytosis (14.2 K/uL) and thrombocytopenia (88-95 K/uL) meet Sepsis-3 criteria for sepsis risk. AKI Stage 3 (creatinine 8.9 mg/dL) indicates severe renal dysfunction. Thrombocytopenia (<100 K/uL) meets SOFA coagulation scoring thresholds. Bilirubin elevation (1.4 mg/dL) and anemia (Hgb 9.8 g/dL) suggest possible hepatic involvement or hemolysis.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and creatinine elevation meet Sepsis-3 criteria (suspected infection + SOFA increase ≥2)
AKI Stage 3 confirmed by creatinine rise ≥1.5x baseline and absolute increase ≥0.3 mg/dL over 48 hours
prioritized_risks.threshold: Creatinine rise ≥0.3 mg/dL in 48h or ≥1.5x baseline in 7d

prioritized_risks.explanation: Platelets <100 K/uL meets SOFA coagulation scoring (threshold: <150 K/uL for SOFA score ≥1)
prioritized_risks.threshold: Platelets <150 K/uL for SOFA coag score ≥1

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L present but vasopressor requirements unknown
prioritized_risks.threshold: Vasopressor-dependent hypotension + lactate >2 mmol/L after fluids

recommended_actions: Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics per sepsis protocol
Order blood cultures and infection source workup
Start renal replacement therapy consultation for AKI Stage 3
Consider platelet transfusion for <50 K/uL (severe coagulopathy threshold)
Repeat creatinine measurement to confirm AKI trajectory
Monitor for signs of disseminated intravascular coagulation (DIC)
doctor_handoff: Review for source control of infection, assess volume status, confirm vasopressor requirements, and evaluate for DIC. Prioritize renal function stabilization.

disease_progression.observation: Baseline abnormalities: WBC 14.2 K/uL, Hgb 9.8 g/dL, Bilirubin 1.4 mg/dL

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.

family_communication.english_summary: In the last 12 hours, your loved one's blood tests show signs of a serious infection (sepsis) affecting multiple organs. Their kidneys are struggling (Stage 3 kidney injury) with very high creatinine levels, and platelet counts remain low, increasing bleeding risk. We've started strong antibiotics, are investigating the infection source, and consulting about kidney support. We're closely watching for worsening kidney function, infection progression, and clotting issues. Next steps include confirming kidney status, managing infection, and supporting organ function.
family_communication.regional_summary: पिछले 12 घंटों में रक्त परीक्षण बताते हैं कि आपके प्रियजन में गंभीर संक्रमण (सेप्सिस) है जो कई अंगों को प्रभावित कर रहा है। उनके गुर्दे काम करने में कठिनाई में हैं (तीसरे चरण की गुर्दा क्षति) और खून के क्रिएटीनीन स्तर बहुत ऊँचे हैं, जबकि प्लेटलेट कम बने हुए हैं जो रक्तस्राव का जोखिम बढ़ाते हैं। हमने शक्तिशाली एंटीबायोटिक शुरू कर दिए हैं, संक्रमण के स्रोत की जांच कर रहे हैं और गुर्दा समर्थन के बारे में परामर्श कर रहे हैं। हम गुर्दे के कार्य में खराबी, संक्रमण के बढ़ने और रक्त जमाव के मुद्दों की निगरानी कर रहे हैं। अगले कदम में गुर्दे की स्थिति की पुष्टि, संक्रमण का प्रबंधन और अंगों के कार्य का समर्थन शामिल है।
